discovery_md:
Reviewed the FC-LS structure and login-related sections across ELS definitions (`4.2.7`, `4.2.8`), login procedures and service parameters (`6.1`-`6.3`), process login/logout (`7.1`-`7.2`), and Virtual Fabrics login negotiation (`8.2`).

Discovered implementation-facing login units:
- Fabric login procedure using FLOGI, including request fields, completion conditions, relogin behavior, and explicit response branches.
- N_Port login procedure using PLOGI, with separate flows for Fabric-present and no-Fabric cases, including collision handling and retry branches.
- Generic login ELS message structure for FLOGI/PLOGI request and LS_ACC reply, with payload delegated to table 149 and service parameters in section 6.6.
- Process login/logout using PRLI/PRLO, including image-pair establishment semantics, PA relationship modes, binding vs informative operation, and PRLI retry/error behavior.
- Explicit logout using LOGO, relevant because PRLI requires prior N_Port login and uses LOGO/LS_RJT when login prerequisites are missing.
- Virtual Fabrics negotiation during FLOGI, including Table 177 behavior of the Virtual Fabrics bit.

Dependencies still unresolved from inspected evidence:
- Login payload table 149 and service-parameter tables 150 and 155 are referenced repeatedly for FLOGI/PLOGI but were not inspected in this run.
- Figures 4 and 5 are referenced to define image and image-pair identification for PRLI, but the figure assets available here did not yield usable raw figure evidence.

unit_candidates.title: Fabric login procedure
unit_candidates.reason: Section 6.2 defines a required login flow with explicit request, response branches, completion conditions, and relogin behavior for establishing Fabric operating characteristics before other Nx_Port communication.

evidence_refs.quote: Login with the Fabric is required for all Nx_Ports, regardless of the class supported.
The explicit Fabric Login procedure shall require an Nx_Port to transmit a Fabric Login (FLOGI) Link
The Nx_Port shall transmit the FLOGI in a new Exchange.
The following are possible responses the Nx_Port may receive when transmitting a FLOGI:
The Originator of the FLOGI request considers Fabric Login to have ended when
The Responder of the FLOGI request considers Fabric Login to have ended when

unit_candidates.title: Fabric login response branches
unit_candidates.reason: The explicit Fabric Login response section enumerates normal Fabric acceptance, point-to-point detection, busy, reject, retry, and self-connection branches, which are implementation-facing control-flow outcomes.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.2.2.3  Responses to Explicit Fabric Login
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
b) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
e) F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-

unit_candidates.title: N_Port login procedure
unit_candidates.reason: Sections 6.3.1-6.3.2 define explicit N_Port Login after Fabric Login, with separate operational flows depending on whether a Fabric is present.

evidence_refs.quote: N_Port Login follows the Fabric Login procedure.
The explicit N_Port Login procedure shall require an Nx_Port to transmit a PLOGI Link request Se-
The destination Nx_Port explicit Login procedure requires transmission of a N_Port Login (PLOGI)

unit_candidates.title: N_Port login responses with Fabric present
unit_candidates.reason: Section 6.3.2.3 enumerates implementation branches for PLOGI with a Fabric present, including accept, Fabric busy/reject, destination busy/reject, collision, LS_RJT, timeout, and self-connection outcomes.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.3.2.3  Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
b) F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
e) P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the N_Port_ID of receiving Nx_Port.
g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.

unit_candidates.title: N_Port login responses with no Fabric present
unit_candidates.reason: Section 6.3.2.5 gives the point-to-point response branches for PLOGI when no Fabric is present, including normal accept, busy/reject branches, collision handling, timeout, and self-connection handling.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.3.2.5  Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
b) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
d) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
e) PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the address identifier of receiving Nx_Port.
No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.
g) If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-

unit_candidates.title: Login ELS message structure for FLOGI and PLOGI
unit_candidates.reason: Section 4.2.7 defines a shared login ELS request/reply structure for FLOGI and PLOGI, while deferring the actual payload layout to table 149 and service-parameter definitions in section 6.6.

evidence_refs.quote: The FLOGI/PLOGI ELS shall transfer Service Parameters from the initiating Nx_Port to the FC_Port
a) FLOGI or PLOGI Request Sequence
The format of the Payload is shown in table 149.  The Service Parameters are defined in
format of the LS_ACC Payload is shown in table 149.  The Service Parameters are defined in 6.6.

unit_candidates.title: Login payload table dependency
unit_candidates.reason: The FLOGI/PLOGI request and LS_ACC reply both cite table 149 as the payload format, but that table was not inspected, so the concrete field layout remains unresolved.

evidence_refs.quote: The format of the Payload is shown in table 149.  The Service Parameters are defined in
format of the LS_ACC Payload is shown in table 149.  The Service Parameters are defined in 6.6.

unit_candidates.title: Login service-parameter applicability tables
unit_candidates.reason: Fabric and N_Port login procedures repeatedly reference applicability tables 150 and 155 for the common and class service parameters, but those tables were not inspected in this run.

evidence_refs.quote: The Service Parameters are as specified for F_Port Login in 6.6.  The applicability of the Service Pa-
rameters to Fabric Login are given in tables 150 and 155.
The N_Port Common Service Parameters during N_Port Login are specified in 6.6.2 (See table 150
for applicability).  The N_Port Class Service Parameters during N_Port Login are specified in 6.6.5
(See table 155 for applicability).

unit_candidates.title: Process login procedure
unit_candidates.reason: Section 7.1 defines PRLI as the explicit procedure for establishing an operating environment and image pairs after N_Port login, with retry/error behavior and separate PA relationship modes.

unit_candidates.title: Process login PA relationships and operating modes
unit_candidates.reason: Sections 7.1.2-7.1.4 define how PRLI pages vary by Originator/Responder Process_Associator validity and by informative versus binding mode, which changes request/response semantics.

unit_candidates.title: PRLI image and image-pair figure dependency
unit_candidates.reason: The PRLI introduction depends on figure-defined concepts for image and image-pair identification, but usable figure evidence was not obtained from the figure assets in this run.

evidence_refs.quote: A group of related processes is known as an image and is identified by the Process_Associator (see
figure 4).  The combination of the D_ID, Responder Process_Associator, S_ID and Originator
Process_Associator identify the image pair (see figure 5).

unit_candidates.title: Process logout procedure
unit_candidates.reason: Section 7.2 defines PRLO as the inverse of PRLI for invalidating an image-pair operating environment, freeing resources, resetting TYPE-specific parameters, and retrying after certain failures.

evidence_refs.quote: The Process Logout (PRLO) ELS request shall be used to request invalidation of the operating envi-
frees resources committed by a previous PRLI function.

unit_candidates.title: Explicit logout procedure
unit_candidates.reason: LOGO is the explicit logout mechanism used to remove service between Nx_Ports or between an Nx_Port and the Fabric, and it is also referenced as a PRLI prerequisite/error response when N_Port login is absent.

unit_candidates.title: Virtual Fabrics negotiation during FLOGI
unit_candidates.reason: Section 8.2 makes FLOGI a login-time negotiation point for the Virtual Fabrics bit and directs behavior, including EVFP initiation when the LS_ACC reply bit is set.

evidence_refs.quote: The use of Virtual Fabrics is negotiated when FLOGI is performed via the Virtual Fabrics bit of the
When set to one in the FLOGI request, the Virtual Fabrics bit indicates that the sending N_Port may
negotiate Virtual Fabrics parameters. When set to one in the FLOGI LS_ACC reply, the Virtual Fab-
rics bit indicates that the Fabric requires the N_Port to initiate an EVFP transaction (see 4.2.43). Ta-

unit_candidates.title: ELS login prerequisite and command codes
unit_candidates.reason: The generic ELS framework identifies which login-related ELSs exist and whether N_Port login is required before use, which matters when sequencing login, process login, and logout operations.
